primary_goal: Build authority for UK ecommerce and wholesale automation through high-quality, specific, operator-led articles.
ai_mention_rule: AI may be covered only when it supports controlled workflows, clear review rules and ROI proof.

articles.title: Your ecommerce operation should survive one person being away
articles.primary_buyer_problem: A critical ecommerce process depends on one employee's undocumented knowledge.
articles.primary_search_intent: An ecommerce or wholesale owner wants to identify and reduce key-person dependency in daily operations.
articles.trend_or_durable_demand: Durable demand with a current 2026 signal. ICAEW updated its spreadsheet competency framework on 2 July 2026 and continues to treat spreadsheet risk, competence and process control as material organisational issues. UK government business-continuity guidance also stresses continuity of critical functions and recovery from disruption.

evidence_sources.source: ICAEW Twenty principles for good spreadsheet practice

articles.what_changed_or_matters: Recent ICAEW material keeps spreadsheet competence and control current for operational and finance teams. The commercial issue is broader than spreadsheet error: undocumented processes create absence risk, bottlenecks and trapped senior capacity.
articles.existing_article_gap: No current article focuses on operational resilience, knowledge silos or the risk created when one employee carries the process.
articles.henry_distinct_contribution: An operator view that competence can hide operational debt, plus a practical five-day absence test for ecommerce workflows.

overlaps_considered: Before you hire another ecommerce admin, check the process first
Ecommerce AI automation: how to prove the ROI first
rolling_12_article_audit.why_distinct: The article is about business continuity and knowledge concentration, not headcount payback or AI ROI. It uses an absence test rather than a standard workflow guide.

articles.original_editorial_asset: Five-day absence test with red, amber and green scoring across tasks, inputs, decisions, exceptions and recovery.
articles.first_hand_contribution: Henry's operator principle that a process is stable only when another capable person can run it safely from available records and rules.
articles.proof_points_used_recently: The 200,000-SKU and eight-hours-to-two-minutes proof points were deliberately not used.
articles.quality_score_justification: The 5 scores reflect a distinct operator argument, original five-day absence test, strong fit with Henry's experience, clear prose and a format not used by the previous article. Evidence and conversion relevance score 4 because the topic is durable rather than strongly transactional or news-led.
articles.hemingway_readability_check: Passed editorial review for grade 9 or lower. Short sentences, active voice and plain operational language were used.
articles.mobile_layout_check: Passed conceptual checks at about 390px and 768px. The scorecard collapses from two columns to one, article width is fluid and CTA text can wrap.
articles.ai_mention_check: Passed. AI is not mentioned because it is not needed for the argument.
articles.search_console_submission: Not submitted; no Search Console tool was available in this runtime.

articles.title: How to reconcile Shopify payouts, orders and fees
How to catch product data errors before listings go live
How to stop re-keying wholesale orders into Sage or Xero
Ecommerce AI automation: how to prove the ROI first

How to automate supplier price lists for Shopify, Amazon and wholesale channels

quality_rules: Write for UK ecommerce and wholesale operators first.
Every article must read at Hemingway grade 9 or lower.
Do not invent results, statistics, dates, names or testimonials.
Every article needs distinct intent and at least one original editorial asset.
AI is one controlled tool, never the offer or a replacement for judgement.
